entries.definition:
h–sh–sh to be brittle; to be pliant, (of bread etc.) to be thin,
to be soft; to be welcoming, to wear a welcoming smile; to drive
away; to beat down tree leaves with a stick; a horse that perspires
too much. Of this root, أَهُشُّ ʾahushshu occurs once in the Qur’an.

أَهُشُّ ʾahushshu [imperf. of v. هَشَّ hashsha] to beat down tree
leaves; to drive away, to restrain (20:18) قَالَ هِيَ عَصَايَ أَتَوَكَّؤُا عَلَيْهَا
وَأَهُشُّ بِهَا عَلَىٰ غَنَمِي he said, ‘It is my staff; I lean on it and I beat
down leaves with it for my sheep (or, I restrain my sheep with it).’
entries.definition_english:
h–sh–sh to be brittle; to be pliant, (of bread etc.) to be thin,
to be soft; to be welcoming, to wear a welcoming smile; to drive
away; to beat down tree leaves with a stick; a horse that perspires
too much. Of this root, أَهُشُّ ʾahushshu occurs once in the Qur’an.

أَهُشُّ ʾahushshu [imperf. of v. هَشَّ hashsha] to beat down tree
leaves; to drive away, to restrain (20:18) قَالَ هِيَ عَصَايَ أَتَوَكَّؤُا عَلَيْهَا
وَأَهُشُّ بِهَا عَلَىٰ غَنَمِي he said, ‘It is my staff; I lean on it and I beat
down leaves with it for my sheep (or, I restrain my sheep with it).’
